title: A temporal and spatial bioinformatics study to analyze the genetic variability among SARS-CoV-2 genomes and design series of nested PCR primers for sensitive diagnosis
author: dewedar, doaa
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.title: During my professional diploma in bioinformatics,...
steps.instruction:
During my professional diploma in bioinformatics, I engaged in a comprehensive study of the genomic variability among SARS-CoV-2 strains. Utilizing multiple sequence alignments and phylogenetic tree construction, we focused on conserved regions and extended our analysis to include genomic variability among SARS-CoV-1 and SARS-CoV-2, including fecal and oropharyngeal isolates. Our research involved an evolutionary comparison with related viral strains, leading to the identification of key SNPs and significant conclusions, such as the phylogenetic relationship of Australian sequences to the original Wuhan strain. 
 Additionally, we delved into the nested PCR primer design for virus detection. Our study revealed that relying solely on C.T. values may not suffice for diagnosing subclinical cases, emphasizing the reliability of PCR for virus detection. We pinpointed critical regions of the virus for molecular identification and recommended nested PCR for highly contaminated samples due to its high sensitivity. This research underscored the importance of well-designed primers and validated the utility of PCR in diagnostics.
